subject: FWD: They’re getting nervous…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scheller for Congress, Inc
body:
Something that Nancy Pelosi said in her email on behalf of Susan Wild, I completely agreed with. You didn’t hear that wrong.... I completely agreed wi Peter, Something that Nancy Pelosi said in her email on behalf of Susan Wild, I completely agreed with.
You didn’t hear that wrong.... I completely agreed with something that Nancy Pelosi said, maybe for the first time ever. [ ](http://links.schellerforcongress.com/u/click?_t=2618b931a0124f90ba4a51cf9fda2c48&_m=b9c8251ecb6c4780b5dd8e3be8659751&_e=Yc7mhED0vvxksrGhtyNk33mC0oOQyYtpUaAYBri5Zzo9JQscuGnvLk1ivCQsfOjyycZbRlPr--V3BFzVRSRkhwc5ZMETQTMu1BSFJwdL8h0KV7lIuNzXiqKBOfQ-vhc0H8BOUzJIzY6RRlMnzdZ2DdFkM7epD7DnXKUaNCbH__a8SkUyfz5z5TY4xtE2-va3GRtYHoMmgaj-dB__A9O9Rum4sgwG5ZvzESOdLClhQgoxfvfQwu5gA6ciBrVuitcGgzoGTUofo9mLYK0DVSDz288zxmifviXEft6dMmk4VW6NpgvIAqf3D12by7me70gur9Xx0fv-xrfEAcqIGylyHD5U5_BUI6wMxe_5CcaZ8OvUPdW3S3QpCL4ZlJ18g2rlTaD-duT4VLwTA-yv4aPKw3tTX7pgq-b382Ok9V0pMSCqX0mcmIUuj9tXKo-DgVzLmuGb0qHfeh5i4RGesXqJ7w%3D%3D) Nancy Pelosi said that the path to winning back the House and flipping it RED runs STRAIGHT through PA-07.
Not only am I confident that we can flip PA-07 red in November, but so is Nancy.
That’s why I need you to immediately chip in to our ‘FLIP PA-07 FUND’ as soon as you can. [**JOIN THE FUND NOW**](http://links.schellerforcongress.com/u/click?_t=2618b931a0124f90ba4a51cf9fda2c48&_m=b9c8251ecb6c4780b5dd8e3be8659751&_e=Yc7mhED0vvxksrGhtyNk33mC0oOQyYtpUaAYBri5Zzo9JQscuGnvLk1ivCQsfOjyycZbRlPr--V3BFzVRSRkhwc5ZMETQTMu1BSFJwdL8h0KV7lIuNzXiqKBOfQ-vhc0H8BOUzJIzY6RRlMnzdZ2DdFkM7epD7DnXKUaNCbH__a8SkUyfz5z5TY4xtE2-va3GRtYHoMmgaj-dB__A9O9Rum4sgwG5ZvzESOdLClhQgquK53TKWZIY_CwNjHs-ipJeKkHqKa67OiJM7kBAyfofyFKmNIy_KVFypcToWHW3h9KxlJq4AyW3XtkebkRNRXnijGyrFu6GVSv86NgkjPaDqbaR8pOBQpYpERfvnfp8ywQSdJgwFw6V1QjrX4bQU6HhSJK7H5pXHDXgymvZem8xfNsnua8jxIf2wVOvGTPIJ4fXFrSxang8wIY_PKK7EZmbDoki5mrvX_Yx_z29WF7QA%3D%3D) [**CHIP IN $25 NOW**](http://links.schellerforcongress.com/u/click?_t=2618b931a0124f90ba4a51cf9fda2c48&_m=b9c8251ecb6c4780b5dd8e3be8659751&_e=Yc7mhED0vvxksrGhtyNk33mC0oOQyYtpUaAYBri5Zzo9JQscuGnvLk1ivCQsfOjyycZbRlPr--V3BFzVRSRkh3JW8Ha2aNk1FBZjkgcoYdE5A2zBD7tCZAv1fFEbCD2i4PUIn2vd1s9UNFV-mOc7QE9UGYwcM0uBF5pNPi6TjetScEU8Iwg6OV8c1pHV8AkSneKcsddjUHxeKN1tgEtdLsh3A0wUEgCyGjluEphtFj5YRQAqPm_wfI0vZB4Qaovl5GkRRxHabXMQ4SFr577TiPaixgC_hS1uL6yCtEEOGw5cAX7POp68M0_HNEPf4HUe0x6UXtDpHOHsDlXzcrLLd1YnCLH1MCr42I7HdkEjUDZFzmBZLA5wwMyr2s7ONPvfRddS3sK1c8drNWRFPC3kO0vu6tdWL3HVrFGoE4PeLc8hXsM6C_Om9j5XZtz3hiw2vp0rf9Qu1F76EK_z9gYDmw%3D%3D) As I have mentioned multiple times previously, this election is ours for the taking. We must continue to keep our voices loud, our name heard, and our campaigning strong.
We must do all we can to keep our taxes low, borders secured, and constitutional rights at the forefront of what we do.
Can I count on you to immediately chip in to ensure the path that DOES run right through PA-07 is in fact flipped red this fall?
Lisa Scheller \-----------Forwarded Message Below---------- Peter, JUST last week, House Speaker and Far-Left Radical Nancy Pelosi sent out an email on behalf of my opponent, Susan Wild.
Simply put, Peter, they’re getting nervous.

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Five Stories President Trump Doesn’t Want You to Miss
body:
Education Secretary Betsy DeVos Wants Schools Open Full-Time This Fall | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ebca56857e&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **_FIVE STORIES PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS_**
---
| | | **[Education Secretary Betsy DeVos Wants Schools Open Full-Time This Fall](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=06541bd308&e=3ee4985eea)**
— _Chicago Tribune_
“Ultimately, it’s not a matter of if schools need to open, it’s a matter of how. School must reopen, they must be fully operational. And how that happens is best left to education and community leaders,” Education Secretary Betsy DeVos told governors this week. On Tuesday, President Trump held a White House summit on reopening schools this fall.
---
|
---
| **[China Never Reported Existence of Coronavirus to World Health Organization](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6e0f575eb8&e=3ee4985eea)**
— _The Washington Free Beacon_
“Contrary to claims from both Chinese officials and the World Health Organization, China did not report the existence of the coronavirus in late 2019, according to a WHO timeline tracking the spread of the virus. Rather, international health officials discovered the virus through information posted to a U.S. website,” Adam Kredo reports.
---
|
---
| **[Last Week's Jobs Report Was Good. Here's Why the July Report Will Be Even Better in Spite of Pandemic Fears.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=489eb89628&e=3ee4985eea)**
— _Yahoo Finance_
“The June employment report from the [Bureau of Labor Statistics] showed an increase of 4.8 million jobs,” _Yahoo Finance_ reports. Now, “daily job listing data, especially in the retail and hospitality sectors,” indicate that July could be an even stronger month for job growth.
---
|
---
| **[Trump Committed to Helping Hispanics Create Prosperity Through Business, Education](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=57a21b0a89&e=3ee4985eea)**
— _Miami Herald_
“As the daughter of first-generation Mexican Americans born in a working-class community in Chicago, I know firsthand how important opportunity is in promoting upward mobility and achieving the American Dream,” writes U.S. Small Business Administrator Jovita Carranza. And creating such opportunity is the “central goal” behind an executive order on Hispanic prosperity that President Trump signed Thursday.
---
|
---
| **[The Media’s War on Words](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4f1e4e010f&e=3ee4985eea)**
— _National Review_
“Every time some new correct-speak emerges, CNN and all the media will participate in browbeating us into subservience. Progressive pundits will laugh off concerns about the Orwellian slippery slope. And all of them will immediately sign on to the next round of rhetorical groupthink and further degrade clear language and clear thinking,” David Harsanyi writes.
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=68f111a2bb&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=537f7cb6f4&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=a02bc213ff)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

On Tuesday, President Trump held a White House summit on reopening schools this fall. | | — The Washington Free Beacon “Contrary to claims from both Chinese officials and the World Health Organization, China did not report the existence of the coronavirus in late 2019, according to a WHO timeline tracking the spread of the virus. Now, “daily job listing data, especially in the retail and hospitality sectors,” indicate that July could be an even stronger month for job growth. | | — Miami Herald “As the daughter of first-generation Mexican Americans born in a working-class community in Chicago, I know firsthand how important opportunity is in promoting upward mobility and achieving the American Dream,” writes U.S. Small Business Administrator Jovita Carranza. And creating such opportunity is the “central goal” behind an executive order on Hispanic prosperity that President Trump signed Thursday. | | — National Review “Every time some new correct-speak emerges, CNN and all the media will participate in browbeating us into subservience. Progressive pundits will laugh off concerns about the Orwellian slippery slope. And all of them will immediately sign on to the next round of rhetorical groupthink and further degrade clear language and clear thinking,” David Harsanyi writes. | | | | | | The White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111

name: Gina Raimondo
subject: Help for housing
body:
Your RI COVID-19 News Update
---
| | | | | |
---
| Hello friend,
There's no question that this crisis has hit hardest for those who can least afford it. If you were just getting by before COVID, chances are you're really struggling now. Many people who have lost jobs in this crisis simply aren't able to make ends meet, and we also know that many jobs that existed pre-COVID-19 may not come back. For many folks, that means not being able to pay rent. Today, I want to tell you about a major initiative we're undertaking to help provide rent relief now.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001lIf2mwe2f8OtsftR6Uhc8sn4cUtz2FdxjZXYI8KrQn25egBiJpm5G06DFuvzjyFAbX05Y0q_k-8T6Oci2uMK6zvcmKdQgcOrHARMP6wN5xZ2e7PeNgRK3n6a-PiD1JLRj7KJWbv4lbakWcsM2d6NjSwcQSa_IC-VbovxiON8LaBfKYjoEFO5R9HHnh6ltm3P3DDHwEJNRgY=&c=ULate41BI0Ku-E4AWuT284VbIPNFejjra3sLnEdtamZhW4-FO_BSwQ==&ch=1xyoICATN4wMbBmBmZNoHSvNmK0ajXH6aHgCb95xJEYznjK86zAV8Q==)
---
| I'm proud to announce that, in coordination with the District Court and in partnership with the United Way, we are launching an eviction diversion effort that we're calling the Safe Harbor Housing Program. This program will serve as an alternative to the traditional eviction process and provide landlords and tenants an opportunity to work towards a solution outside of the court system.
We're going to be putting $7 million of CARES Act funding into this effort, which we expect will support between 1,000 and 2,000 Rhode Islanders to maintain stable housing. Here's how it will work:

* Renters who meet income criteria and are behind on their rent because of the COVID-19 emergency will be able to qualify for assistance. Tenants can apply directly, or landlords can apply on behalf of tenants who need help getting back on their feet. Starting Monday, you can contact United Way and ask about the Safe Harbor program to start the application process.
* If both parties agree to participate, they'll be contacted by a United Way housing specialist to finalize a payment agreement supported by CARES Act funding. Tenants who need legal advice will be able to access free legal services.
* To be clear, you can apply to participate before you receive an eviction notice, if you don't think you'll be able to catch up on the rent you owe. We want to resolve these situations early to the extent possible.

I want to thank the District Court and the United Way for their collaboration in this process. Once the program launches on Monday, you can go to the United Way website, [www.uwri.org](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001lIf2mwe2f8OtsftR6Uhc8sn4cUtz2FdxjZXYI8KrQn25egBiJpm5G06DFuvzjyFAma4FjjPXtj40k2U1GmO_L0a9TN9MNlEOxl_LS3ycuu3VeUiXRDRbg5yN3eUnMPqk9JHentrmoIQ=&c=ULate41BI0Ku-E4AWuT284VbIPNFejjra3sLnEdtamZhW4-FO_BSwQ==&ch=1xyoICATN4wMbBmBmZNoHSvNmK0ajXH6aHgCb95xJEYznjK86zAV8Q==), or call 211 to apply.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001lIf2mwe2f8OtsftR6Uhc8sn4cUtz2FdxjZXYI8KrQn25egBiJpm5G7HjfWPUrPHciYoDwbLX6NPQwNvMMfb_Rxm9GBl6nWUjt6YGQqtv-Q6mzBdH4b0K2b5aHZatD2JwN7T5bNcTunNcVGxQY8qECCqr0NXA_wGoxpyIY7hKv4VV4l09VyB1GSGtl3rEJnxTsv13Ze4GQIw=&c=ULate41BI0Ku-E4AWuT284VbIPNFejjra3sLnEdtamZhW4-FO_BSwQ==&ch=1xyoICATN4wMbBmBmZNoHSvNmK0ajXH6aHgCb95xJEYznjK86zAV8Q==)
---
| Click the graph to see check the Department of Health's [COVID-19 data dashboard](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001lIf2mwe2f8OtsftR6Uhc8sn4cUtz2FdxjZXYI8KrQn25egBiJpm5G7HjfWPUrPHciYoDwbLX6NPQwNvMMfb_Rxm9GBl6nWUjt6YGQqtv-Q6mzBdH4b0K2b5aHZatD2JwN7T5bNcTunNcVGxQY8qECCqr0NXA_wGoxpyIY7hKv4VV4l09VyB1GSGtl3rEJnxTsv13Ze4GQIw=&c=ULate41BI0Ku-E4AWuT284VbIPNFejjra3sLnEdtamZhW4-FO_BSwQ==&ch=1xyoICATN4wMbBmBmZNoHSvNmK0ajXH6aHgCb95xJEYznjK86zAV8Q==).
---
| I want to be clear that this is not going to completely solve the uphill battle that we face. However. it is an important stopgap that will help Rhode Islanders who are most in need work through a short-term solution while they get back on their feet. A permanent solution is going to require legislative action, and I look forward to working with the General Assembly to make it happen.
We also need legislation to prohibit discrimination based on source of income. Currently in Rhode Island it's not against the law to refuse to rent to someone because they're paying for their rent with a housing voucher. That's wrong, and we need to pass [the legislation that's currently pending in the House](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001lIf2mwe2f8OtsftR6Uhc8sn4cUtz2FdxjZXYI8KrQn25egBiJpm5G06DFuvzjyFAovF6WmjXXkjzY8WUsaA_xYZKluam1tOILI5Gmhdfad-qrv95ORqlSd6fa7bAj7652eXwb83oPq5AL-c61TZ3fTnv18gPkGdJ5XjxXjoDfszWOEgHTLb_rmTe0Klop7L5z9KJlhq6iRVv0wp_c5aShk-GgUQxFLq6&c=ULate41BI0Ku-E4AWuT284VbIPNFejjra3sLnEdtamZhW4-FO_BSwQ==&ch=1xyoICATN4wMbBmBmZNoHSvNmK0ajXH6aHgCb95xJEYznjK86zAV8Q==) to fix it.
-Gina Raimondo Governor
P.S.: Information is our greatest weapon in the fight against COVID-19, If you or someone you know wants to sign up to receive these emails, you can use [this link](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001lIf2mwe2f8OtsftR6Uhc8sn4cUtz2FdxjZXYI8KrQn25egBiJpm5G06DFuvzjyFAV6fhTG6n2BQxCJVIC-Ken5EX1indFVukmI6OJk7j8Mmgn_bWGP2kI3w0xMSs_FQC4WoWwfUI2yKkB9Zji1XLLnL3GRNmpqq-3q8ejoVHKi3S_GmsOI-qc0VOJ85GFs8GCWP8ov5AMsafFv0dI3H0McikGArUzl4l_BcP8DBs70n9FNvh_1VaQQmXvBbu_hUHYqJ670sPI0yUhktPqmFuuk6O6cy7zTtTG8XDVmO7tOruSJifUm-Pq-Z-Q1C1E93YtCXmkgIafjiLfIEH5vug7_IIdXx_FabHOLytLYGtMgGizsjfS3zxlD56FdQHQTRNq5mfGpYQ11dHyxybSXPGm80cmd4Mkf5rQcGMAwUiuvnl_l7SmoqqTqfGX0PLjJ9x&c=ULate41BI0Ku-E4AWuT284VbIPNFejjra3sLnEdtamZhW4-FO_BSwQ==&ch=1xyoICATN4wMbBmBmZNoHSvNmK0ajXH6aHgCb95xJEYznjK86zAV8Q==).
---

Sent by [communications@governor.ri.gov](mailto:communications@governor.ri.gov) in collaboration with

name: Team Christy
subject: Asking for your support -- but not for us.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress
body:
| []()
---

| | Times are tough, but Californians are tougher.

**We can get through this pandemic by sticking together, Peter.** That's why Team Christy is attending a socially-distanced food distribution event this weekend sponsored by Bridge to Home, a Santa Clarita-based non-profit that provides assistance to folks experiencing homelessness.

**[If you want to help our neighbors who've been most affected by the COVID-19 pandemic, please make a donation of any size to Bridge to Home so they can get folks the support they need.](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bae2/29776869/9ef5eb9/569ee388/3462122771/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTBfYnJpZGdlLWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cs-bridge2home?refcode=em20_200710_bridge-full")** [DONATE TO BRIDGE TO HOME](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bae2/29776869/9ef5eb9/569ee388/3462122771/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTBfYnJpZGdlLWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cs-bridge2home?refcode=em20_200710_bridge-full") Bridge to Home has helped thousands of families get back on track over the last 19 years. They provide dozens of shelter beds, evening meals, eviction protection, employment assistance, and more to help our neighbors who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness.

With millions of Americans losing their jobs or having their hours cut, the need for Bridge to Home's services has only grown.

Christy and our team know how important it is to support this work directly. Christy knows first-hand what it's like to not know where your next meal is coming from or if you'll be able to stay in your home. As a kid, her family struggled after her parents split, and while her mom worked full-time, it wasn't always enough.

We can and must end hunger and homelessness -- through policies, yes, but also community programs.

**[That's why we hope you'll make a donation to Bridge to Home today and support our Southern California neighbors.](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bae2/29776869/9ef5eb9/569ee388/3462122771/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTBfYnJpZGdlLWZ1bGwifQ%3D%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cs-bridge2home?refcode=em20_200710_bridge-full")**

Thank you,

Team Christy

clean_body: | []() | | Times are tough, but Californians are tougher. We can get through this pandemic by sticking together, Peter. That's why Team Christy is attending a socially-distanced food distribution event this weekend sponsored by Bridge to Home, a Santa Clarita-based non-profit that provides assistance to folks experiencing homelessness. "" "" Bridge to Home has helped thousands of families get back on track over the last 19 years. They provide dozens of shelter beds, evening meals, eviction protection, employment assistance, and more to help our neighbors who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness. With millions of Americans losing their jobs or having their hours cut, the need for Bridge to Home's services has only grown. Christy and our team know how important it is to support this work directly. Christy knows first-hand what it's like to not know where your next meal is coming from or if you'll be able to stay in your home. As a kid, her family struggled after her parents split, and while her mom worked full-time, it wasn't always enough. We can and must end hunger and homelessness -- through policies, yes, but also community programs. "" Thank you, Team Christy | ---|---|--- | | Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress. ---|--- |

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: Things are bad, and getting worse
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
body:
Things are bad, and getting worse.

The question is, what are we going to do about it?

My view is that, in order to make it better in the long term, we have to
win elections. Put people in office who believe in science, follow the
facts, and do what is right, regardless of the politics. That’s why I’m
hosting a [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42652?source=ema20200710-97066&t=1001&akid=97066%2E444211%2EmhQj0n ]phone bank this next Thursday, July 16th, where we will reach
out to hundreds of thousands of Texas Democrats. The results from these
calls will be shared with the Texas Democratic Party as well as Democratic
candidates up and down the ballot (courthouse to state house to White
House) to use for Get Out the Vote efforts in the fall.

In the midst of this pandemic, unable to knock on doors, hold rallies and
town halls, and totally dependent on phone-to-phone field work, Texas
Democratic candidates will be able to leverage our work to contact their
voters. They won’t have to waste time calling bad numbers or Trump
supporters, because our volunteers will have made the millions of calls
necessary to cull extraneous numbers and return a verified, usable list of
Texas Democrats that we can turn out during early voting and on Election
Day.

That’s why we’re doing this work now, to win these elections in November.

We don’t want to look back on this day from election night wondering
whether we could have done more. Let’s do everything we can now.

As if we needed any additional clarity on the stakes of this election,
yesterday we learned that Texas had reached a new record for Covid-19
deaths and hospitalizations, and that the United States recorded its
highest single-day spike in new cases.

In Corpus Christi, they’ve run out of room in the morgue and they’re
requesting morgue trailers from FEMA. In the Rio Grande Valley they’ve run
out of nurses. El Paso, Dallas, San Antonio, Austin and Houston are all
seeing record spikes in transmission, hospitalizations and death.

These are the consequences of past elections.

America elected a President who is contemptuous of public health guidance.
The man in the most powerful position of public trust ignores the
scientists and doctors whose advice might have kept us safe. He instead
chooses to traffic in lies, create chaos and confusion, leaving us the
country hardest hit by the virus. Americans represent 4% of the global
population but we are now 25% of the world’s cases and deaths.

Texas elected a Governor who, in the words of the Houston Chronicle,
chooses to “embrace partisan politics and a fringe populist backlash over
common sense and sound medical advice from his own advisers.” Governor
Abbott has “condemned the Lone Star State to the circumstances we face
today: Hospitals near the breaking point. Nearly 3,000 Texans dead — and
counting.”

But if it is the elections of these men that left us in such desperate
straights, it’s the elections in November that can begin to make things
right.

Electing a Democratic majority to the state house for the first time in 20
years puts people who believe in science, in expanding access to
healthcare, in addressing the disproportionate burden that Black and
Latino Texans carry in this crisis into power. And awarding our 38
electoral college votes to the Democratic nominee for President for the
first time in over forty years spells the end of Trump and the beginning
of something much better.

But the only way to make these changes, the only way to win these
elections, is through the hard work of contacting the voters who will
decide them. That’s what we do and we’d love to have your help doing it.

Please join us:

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42652?source=ema20200710-97066&t=1002&akid=97066%2E444211%2EmhQj0n ]Virtual Voter Contact Phone Bank with Beto
Thursday, July 16
7 p.m. - 9 p.m. CT

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/42652?source=ema20200710-97066&t=1003&akid=97066%2E444211%2EmhQj0n ]RSVP now

Thank you,

Beto

This is a picture from last Monday’s phone bank, where we made over
316,000 phone calls.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1004&akid=97066%2E444211%2EmhQj0n

PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
